title: Ecolier Practice Set 12 — Mixed Review
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official competition questions.

questions.question_text: What is 6 x 7?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='10' y='10' width='100' height='60'/></g><text x='60' y='48' text-anchor='middle' font-size='22' fill='currentColor'>6 x 7 = ?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A box displaying the expression 6 x 7.

questions.question_text: A baker has 48 rolls and packs them in bags of 6. How many bags does the baker fill?
A pizza is divided into 8 equal slices. Max eats 3 slices. What fraction of the pizza is left?
questions.image_description: A circle divided into 8 equal slices.

questions.question_text: A rectangle has length 9 cm and width 4 cm. What is its perimeter?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='20' y='20' width='90' height='40'/></g><text x='65' y='15' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>9 cm</text><text x='115' y='44' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>4 cm</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle with length 9 cm and width 4 cm.

questions.question_text: A movie starts at 2:45 pm and lasts 1 hour 30 minutes. What time does it end?
In the number 5348, what is the value of the digit 3?
questions.solution: The 3 is in the hundreds place, so its value is 300.

questions.question_text: Lara buys a notebook for $4 and a pen for $2. She pays with a $10 bill. How much change does she get?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='10' y='15' width='60' height='30'/><rect x='90' y='15' width='60' height='30'/></g><text x='40' y='34' text-anchor='middle' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>$4</text><text x='120' y='34' text-anchor='middle' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>$2</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Two price tags: $4 for a notebook and $2 for a pen.

questions.question_text: What is the next number in the pattern: 4, 9, 14, 19, ___?
A rectangle has length 7 cm and width 3 cm. What is its area?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 70' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='15' y='15' width='70' height='30'/></g><text x='50' y='55' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>7 cm</text><text x='92' y='34' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>3 cm</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle measuring 7 cm by 3 cm.

questions.question_text: A classroom has 4 shelves, each holding 15 books. The teacher removes 12 books. How many books remain?
Which fraction is equal to 1/2?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 40' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='10' y='10' width='60' height='20'/><rect x='10' y='10' width='30' height='20'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A bar split in half with one half shaded.

questions.question_text: A train leaves at 9:20 am and arrives at 11:05 am. How long is the trip?
questions.solution: From 9:20 to 11:20 is 2 hours; subtract 15 min to reach 11:05, giving 1 h 45 min.

questions.question_text: When 29 is divided by 4, what is the remainder?
How many small squares are in the 3 by 3 grid?
questions.image_description: A 3 by 3 grid of small squares.

questions.question_text: Kate has 45 stickers. She gives 12 to her brother and buys 8 more. How many does she have now?
An L-shaped figure has outer sides measuring 6, 4, 3, 2, 3, and 2 units in order around the shape. What is its perimeter?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><path d='M10 10 L70 10 L70 50 L40 50 L40 70 L10 70 Z'/></g></svg>

questions.question_text: A fruit vendor sells apples for $2 each and pears for $3 each. A customer buys 5 apples and 4 pears. What is the total cost?
A square has a perimeter of 32 cm. What is its area?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='20' y='20' width='60' height='60'/></g></svg>
questions.solution: Side = 32/4 = 8 cm, so area = 8 x 8 = 64 sq cm.

questions.question_text: Three friends have ages that add to 24. The youngest is 6 and the oldest is twice the youngest. How old is the middle friend?
A rope 84 cm long is cut into pieces of 7 cm each. How many pieces are there?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 30' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='10' y='10' width='180' height='10'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A long rope to be cut into equal pieces.

questions.question_text: A jug holds 3/4 of a liter of juice. If 1/4 liter is poured out, how much juice is left?
A school bus carries 36 students. On a field trip, 3 buses are full and a fourth bus is half full. How many students are on the trip?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='10' y='20' width='40' height='25'/><rect x='55' y='20' width='40' height='25'/><rect x='100' y='20' width='40' height='25'/><rect x='145' y='20' width='40' height='25'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Four school buses in a row.

questions.question_text: Two whole numbers have a sum of 15 and a product of 54. What is the larger number?
questions.solution: 6 + 9 = 15 and 6 x 9 = 54, so the larger is 9.

questions.question_text: A box contains red and blue marbles in a ratio of 2 red for every 3 blue. If there are 10 red marbles, how many marbles are in the box in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><circle cx='20' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='40' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='70' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='90' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='110' cy='30' r='8'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Marbles showing a 2 to 3 ratio pattern.
questions.solution: 10 red means 5 groups of 2, so 5 x 3 = 15 blue, total = 10 + 15 = 25.
